cards.title: Candy Medicine
cards.body: Some medications are designed to taste like candy to improve patient compliance.

cards.title: Wombat Poop
cards.body: Wombats produce cubic-shaped scat, which helps their droppings stay put on rocky terrain.

cards.title: Unicorn Nation
cards.body: The unicorn is Scotland's national animal, dating back to the 12th century.

cards.title: Honey Never Spoils
cards.body: Honey's unique properties make it impossible for it to spoil, thanks to its low water content.

cards.title: Dream Memory
cards.body: Research suggests that we tend to forget up to 90% of our dreams within the first few minutes of waking.

cards.title: Chewing Sound
cards.body: The sound of chewing can trigger a response in the brain associated with hunger.

sources.title: Neural basis of food perception
sources.publisher: National Institute of Diabetes and Digestive and Kidney Diseases

cards.title: Taste Smell Connection
cards.body: Our sense of smell plays a significant role in our perception of taste.

sources.title: Smell and Taste
sources.publisher: National Institute on Deafness and Other Communication Disorders

cards.title: Colorful Animals
cards.body: Some species of frogs can change their skin color to match their surroundings.

cards.title: Sea Cucumber Regeneration
cards.body: Some sea cucumbers can expel their internal organs as a defense mechanism and then regrow them.

cards.title: Giraffe Neck
cards.body: Giraffes have the highest blood pressure of any animal, which helps them pump blood up to their brains against gravity.

cards.title: Dancing Peacock Spiders
cards.body: Male peacock spiders perform complex courtship dances to attract females.

cards.title: Zombie Fungus
cards.body: The Ophiocordyceps unilateralis fungus infects ants and controls their behavior, causing them to climb to high locations before producing spores.

cards.title: Vampire Squid
cards.body: Vampire squid have a unique ability to turn their skin into a 'cloak of darkness' by expanding their skin cells to cover their bioluminescent spots.

cards.title: Electric Eels
cards.body: Electric eels can generate up to 600 volts of electricity, which they use for self-defense and hunting.

cards.title: Glowing Waves
cards.body: Certain types of phytoplankton can make waves glow in the dark, a phenomenon known as 'sea sparkles'.

cards.title: Butterfly Taste
cards.body: Butterflies taste with their feet, which helps them detect the sweetness or bitterness of a substance.

cards.title: Sea Pig
cards.body: The sea pig is a type of sea cucumber that uses its many tentacles to capture food particles from the seafloor.

cards.title: Dumbo Octopus
cards.body: The Dumbo octopus has ear-like fins that resemble the ears of Disney's Dumbo the elephant.

cards.title: Axolotl Regeneration
cards.body: Axolotls have the unique ability to regrow their limbs, eyes, and parts of their brain, making them a subject of scientific interest.

cards.title: Narwhal Tusk
cards.body: The narwhal's tusk is actually a modified tooth that can grow up to 10 feet long.

cards.title: Mantis Shrimp
cards.body: The mantis shrimp has the most advanced eyes of any animal, with the ability to see up to 12 different colors and perceive polarized light.

cards.title: Star-Nosed Mole
cards.body: The star-nosed mole has 22 fleshy tentacles on its snout that it uses to detect seismic vibrations in the ground.

cards.title: Blobfish
cards.body: The blobfish is a deep-sea fish that has a soft, gelatinous body and a head that is surrounded by a floating mass of flesh.

cards.title: Aye-Aye
cards.body: The aye-aye is a type of lemur that has a long, thin middle finger that it uses to catch insects and grubs.

cards.title: Tarsier Eyes
cards.body: The tarsier has the largest eyes of any mammal relative to its body size, with eyes that are fixed in place and can only move by rotating its head.

cards.title: Duck-Billed Platypus
cards.body: The duck-billed platypus is one of the few venomous mammals, with males having a venomous spur on their hind legs.

quizzes.question: What shape is wombat scat?
quizzes.explanation: Wombat scat is cubic in shape, which helps it stay in place on rocky terrain.

quizzes.question: How much of our dreams do we tend to forget?
quizzes.explanation: Research suggests that we tend to forget up to 90% of our dreams within the first few minutes of waking.

quizzes.question: What is unique about the vampire squid's skin?
options: It expands to cover bioluminescent spots
quizzes.explanation: The vampire squid has a unique ability to expand its skin cells to cover its bioluminescent spots, turning its skin into a 'cloak of darkness'.

quizzes.question: What do butterflies use to taste?
quizzes.explanation: Butterflies taste with their feet, which helps them detect the sweetness or bitterness of a substance.

quizzes.question: What is unique about the axolotl's abilities?
quizzes.explanation: The axolotl has the unique ability to regrow its limbs, eyes, and parts of its brain, making it a subject of scientific interest.

quizzes.question: What is the narwhal's tusk actually?
quizzes.explanation: The narwhal's tusk is actually a modified tooth that can grow up to 10 feet long.

quizzes.question: What is unique about the star-nosed mole's snout?
options: It has a long, thin nose
It has a flat, disk-like shape
It has a pointed, triangular shape
quizzes.explanation: The star-nosed mole has 22 fleshy tentacles on its snout that it uses to detect seismic vibrations in the ground.

quizzes.question: What is the duck-billed platypus?
quizzes.explanation: The duck-billed platypus is one of the few venomous mammals, with males having a venomous spur on their hind legs.
